title: bZx flash loan attack — price oracle manipulation via flash-loan
summary: bZx protocol lost $8M via flash-loan-based price oracle manipulation. The attacker used a flash loan to artificially inflate token prices within a single transaction, then exploited lending protocols relying on on-chain price feeds.

title: Harvest Finance — flash loan oracle manipulation
summary: Harvest Finance lost $34M when an attacker used a flash loan to manipulate the Curve Finance price oracle, draining funds from the yUSD vault.

title: Cream Finance — reentrancy + price manipulation
summary: Cream Finance lost $130M in a reentrancy attack that exploited ERC777 token callbacks during flash loan processing, combined with oracle price manipulation.

title: BurgerSwap — flash loan reentrancy on BSC
summary: BurgerSwap lost $6.2M when a flash loan was used to trigger a reentrancy via ERC777 token callbacks, allowing the attacker to withdraw funds before the balance was updated.

title: Curve Finance — Vyper reentrancy exploit
summary: Curve Finance pools deployed with Vyper 0.2.15-0.3.0 had a reentrancy bug in the Vyper compiler, allowing recursive calls during token transfers that drained ~$70M across multiple pools.

title: The DAO — reentrancy attack, $60M ETH stolen
summary: The DAO reentrancy attack exploited the withdrawal pattern to recursively call the fallback function before balances were updated, stealing 3.6M ETH. Led to the Ethereum hard fork.

title: Euler Finance — flash loan attack with oracle manipulation
summary: Euler Finance lost $197M in a complex attack using flash loans combined with oracle manipulation to exploit the lending protocol's liquidation mechanisms.

title: Sonne Finance — read-only reentrancy on Compound fork
summary: Sonne Finance lost $2.1M through read-only reentrancy on their Compound v2 fork, where the oracle callback allowed stale balance reads during a flash loan.

title: Cashio — broken mint authority, unlimited token minting
summary: Cashio lost $52M when an attacker discovered that the mint authority validation was broken, allowing anyone to mint CASH tokens without backing collateral. A Solana access-control failure.

title: Solend — oracle manipulation risk via emergency governance
summary: Solend faced a governance crisis where emergency powers could manipulate oracle prices. The protocol used centralized oracle inputs that could be overridden via governance, creating systemic risk.

title: Generic pattern — upgradeable proxy admin takeover
summary: Upgradeable proxy patterns require strict admin authority management. If the upgrade authority or proxy admin can be transferred without proper timelock or multi-sig, a compromised key can deploy arbitrary logic.

title: Wormhole Bridge — unvalidated CPI / missing signer check ($326M)
summary: Wormhole Bridge lost $326M when a validator compromise allowed minting of 120k wETH without proper signature validation on the Solana side. The CPI call to the Solana program lacked proper signer verification.

title: Mango Markets — oracle manipulation via spot price ($114M)
summary: Mango Markets lost $114M when an attacker manipulated the protocol's oracle to inflate collateral values, enabling unauthorized withdrawals. The spot price oracle was manipulated through a series of large trades.

title: Ronin Bridge — validator key compromise ($625M)
summary: Ronin Bridge lost $625M when 5 out of 9 validator keys were compromised, allowing the attacker to approve fraudulent withdrawals. The key management and multi-sig threshold were insufficient.

title: Nomad Bridge — bridge fake proof ($190M)
summary: Nomad Bridge lost $190M when a routine upgrade left the bridge in a state where any message could be accepted as valid. A fraudulent proof was accepted because the validation check was not properly enforced after the upgrade.

title: Beanstalk — governance flash loan attack ($182M)
summary: Beanstalk lost $182M when an attacker used flash loans to acquire enough governance tokens to pass a malicious proposal, draining the protocol's reserves in a single transaction.

title: Compound — oracle price feed manipulation ($80M)
summary: Compound suffered an oracle-related incident where the price feed could be manipulated, leading to incorrect liquidations and token distribution errors affecting approximately $80M in value.

title: BEC Token — integer overflow ($900M notional)
summary: The Beauty Chain (BEC) token had a batchTransfer function vulnerable to integer overflow, allowing an attacker to mint quadrillions of tokens. The overflow in the multiplication of amount and the number of recipients bypassed the balance check.

title: Wintermute — private key compromise via vanity address ($160M)
summary: Wintermute lost $160M in DeFi tokens when their hot wallet private key was compromised. The key was associated with a vanity address that may have been generated using a weak RNG, leading to key extraction.
